div.h1: epoll: Interest Set vs. Readiness Queue (Kernel Data Structures)

g.text: epoll_ctl(epfd, ADD, fd, &ev) → interest set insert
epoll_ctl(epfd, MOD, fd, &ev) → interest set update
epoll_ctl(epfd, DEL, fd, NULL)→ interest set remove
epoll_wait(epfd, events, n, t)→ drain readiness queue
INTEREST SET — Red-Black Tree (O(log n) lookup)
4. Kernel walks socket wait_queue → finds epitem
6. wake_up() unblocks thread sleeping in epoll_wait
Wait for event | O(n) rescan | O(1) sleep
Event delivery | O(n) rescan | O(1) append
Return results | O(n) copy | O(ready) copy
n=10000, 50 ready: select rescans 10000; epoll copies 50
Lookup: O(log n) by fd key
Stored in kernel, NOT user memory
rdllist: doubly-linked list of ready epitem*
Only fds with PENDING events appear here
Kernel appends when socket buffer changes
epoll_wait copies and clears in O(ready) time
LT mode: re-adds if buffer still non-empty
ET mode: NOT re-added — one event per transition
returns nready=2 (fd=3, fd=99 idle — not here)
epoll_ctl ADD/MOD/DEL | write to interest set
epoll_wait() call | blocks thread until ready
